/* * Copyright 1999-2025 Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. * * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License. * You may obtain a copy of the License at * * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 * * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and * limitations under the License. */ package com.alibaba.nacos.console.handler.ai; import com.alibaba.nacos.api.ai.model.mcp.McpEndpointSpec; import com.alibaba.nacos.api.ai.model.mcp.McpServerBasicInfo; import com.alibaba.nacos.api.ai.model.mcp.McpServerDetailInfo; import com.alibaba.nacos.api.ai.model.mcp.McpTool; import com.alibaba.nacos.api.ai.model.mcp.McpToolSpecification; import com.alibaba.nacos.api.exception.NacosException; import com.alibaba.nacos.api.model.Page; /** * Actual Handler class for handling AI MCP operations. * * @author xiweng.yy */ public interface McpHandler { /** * List mcp server. * * @param namespaceId namespace id of mcp servers * @param mcpName mcp name pattern, if null or empty, filter all mcp servers. * @param search search type `blur` or `accurate`, means whether to search by fuzzy or exact match by * `mcpName`. * @param pageNo page number, start from 1 * @param pageSize page size each page * @return list of {@link McpServerBasicInfo} matched input parameters. * @throws NacosException any exception during handling */ Page listMcpServers(String namespaceId, String mcpName, String search, int pageNo, int pageSize) throws NacosException; /** * Get specified mcp server detail info. * * @param namespaceId namespace id of mcp server * @param mcpName name of mcp server * @param mcpId id of mcp server * @param version version of the mcp server * @return detail info with {@link McpServerDetailInfo} * @throws NacosException any exception during handling */ McpServerDetailInfo getMcpServer(String namespaceId, String mcpName, String mcpId, String version) throws NacosException; /** * Create new mcp server. * * @param namespaceId namespace id of mcp server * @param serverSpecification mcp server specification, see {@link McpServerBasicInfo} * @param toolSpecification mcp server included tools, see {@link McpTool}, optional * @param endpointSpecification mcp server endpoint specification, see {@link McpEndpointSpec}, optional * @return mcp server id of the new mcp server * @throws NacosException any exception during handling */ String createMcpServer(String namespaceId, McpServerBasicInfo serverSpecification, McpToolSpecification toolSpecification, McpEndpointSpec endpointSpecification) throws NacosException; /** * Update existed mcp server. * *

* `namespaceId` and `mcpName` can't be changed. *

* * @param namespaceId namespace id of mcp server, used to mark which mcp server to update * @param isPublish publish the current version to latest * @param serverSpecification mcp server specification, see {@link McpServerBasicInfo} * @param toolSpecification mcp server included tools, see {@link McpTool}, optional * @param endpointSpecification mcp server endpoint specification, see {@link McpEndpointSpec}, optional * @throws NacosException any exception during handling */ void updateMcpServer(String namespaceId, boolean isPublish, McpServerBasicInfo serverSpecification, McpToolSpecification toolSpecification, McpEndpointSpec endpointSpecification) throws NacosException; /** * Delete existed mcp server. * * @param namespaceId namespace id of mcp server * @param mcpId id of the mcp server * @param version version of the mcp server * @param mcpName name of mcp server * @throws NacosException any exception during handling */ void deleteMcpServer(String namespaceId, String mcpName, String mcpId, String version) throws NacosException; }

content.text: ## What I found (MCP server config + auth/securitySchemes + “Higress OpenAPI” path) ### 1) Where MCP server config is stored/parsed in Nacos (backend) Nacos “managed MCP server” config is stored as JSON specs and parsed from request forms here: - **Parse server/tool/endpoint specs from incoming JSON** - `/testbed/ai/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/ai/utils/McpRequestUtil.java` - `parseMcpServerBasicInfo()` → deserializes `serverSpecification` - `parseMcpTools()` → deserializes `toolSpecification` - `parseMcpEndpointSpec()` → deserializes `endpointSpecification` - **Create/update MCP server configs in Nacos config store** - `/testbed/ai/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/ai/service/McpServerOperationService.java` - `createMcpServer(...)` / `updateMcpServer(...)` - `injectToolAndEndpoint(...)` persists tool spec via `toolOperationService.refreshMcpTool(...)` and endpoint via `endpointOperationService.createMcpServerEndpointServiceIfNecessary(...)` These backend models do **not** include anything like `securitySchemes` or passthrough auth config: - `/testbed/api/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/api/ai/model/mcp/McpEndpointSpec.java` (only `type` + `data` map) - `/testbed/api/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/api/ai/model/mcp/McpServerRemoteServiceConfig.java` (serviceRef/exportPath/frontEndpointConfigList) - `/testbed/api/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/api/ai/model/mcp/FrontEndpointConfig.java` (front endpoint routing info) ### 2) Where “managed Nacos MCP server config is parsed/converted to Higress OpenAPI spec” I could **not** find any backend converter that turns Nacos managed MCP config into a “Higress OpenAPI spec”. Search results: - No Java code in `/testbed/ai/src/main/java` matching `Higress`, `toOpenAPI`, `convert.*openapi`, etc. - The only `Higress` / `openapi route` hit in the repo was a built static bundle: - `/testbed/console/src/main/resources/static/js/main.js` (minified frontend bundle; not a clear source-of-truth for conversion logic) What *does* exist is a **frontend-only** OpenAPI → MCP-tools extractor: - `/testbed/console-ui/src/pages/AI/McpDetail/Swagger2Tools.js` - `extractToolsFromOpenAPI(openapi)` parses: - `openapi.components.securitySchemes` into `mcpConfig.server.securitySchemes` - `openapi.paths` into tool list - `operation.security` into `tool.requestTemplate.security = { id: schemeName }` So the OpenAPI parsing/conversion is happening in the **console UI**, not in backend Nacos services (at least in this codebase snapshot). ### 3) Where passthrough authentication handling exists today The only explicit “passthrough-like” behavior I found is when the Console calls an MCP server directly to import tools, optionally passing an auth token: - `/testbed/console/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/console/controller/v3/ai/ConsoleMcpController.java` - endpoint: `GET /v3/console/ai/mcp/importToolsFromMcp` - if `authToken` provided: ```java transportBuilder.customizeRequest(req -> req.header("Authorization", "Bearer " + authToken)); ``` This is **not** OpenAPI securitySchemes; it’s just a one-off Bearer header injection for that import call. ### 4) Why `securitySchemes` “cannot be set” (most likely) There’s no backend model field or persistence path for `securitySchemes` in managed MCP server config. Concretely: - UI’s OpenAPI import produces an object shaped like: - `mcpConfig.server.securitySchemes = [...]` (from `Swagger2Tools.js`) - But when creating/updating a server, the UI only sends: - `serverSpecification` - `toolSpecification` - `endpointSpecification` as JSON strings (no securitySchemes field anywhere). Example: - `/testbed/console-ui/src/pages/AI/NewMcpServer/NewMcpServer.js` builds `params` with: ```js params = { serverSpecification: JSON.stringify({...}), toolSpecification: JSON.stringify(this.state?.serverConfig?.toolSpec || {}), endpointSpecification: JSON.stringify({...}) // if not stdio } ``` No `securitySchemes` is included. Also, in the backend, `McpServerBasicInfo` / `McpServerStorageInfo` (used by `McpServerOperationService`) are the only stored “server spec” objects; since they don’t carry `securitySchemes`, even if UI sent them, they’d be dropped on deserialization unless those classes already had matching fields (not seen in the inspected files). ## Likely fix location(s) Depending on desired behavior: 1) **If managed MCP server should persist securitySchemes/passthrough auth config** - Backend model + persistence needs to be extended. The most central place is: - `/testbed/ai/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/ai/service/McpServerOperationService.java` - It persists `serverSpecification` JSON (`buildMcpConfigForm(...).setContent(JacksonUtils.toJson(serverSpecification))`) - And the parsing entrypoint is: - `/testbed/ai/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/ai/utils/McpRequestUtil.java` - `parseMcpServerBasicInfo()` would need to deserialize into a model that includes the new fields. 2) **If securitySchemes is only needed for “Higress OpenAPI generation”** - There is no visible backend “Nacos MCP → Higress OpenAPI” generator in this repo snapshot; the conversion you have today is **OpenAPI → MCP** in: - `/testbed/console-ui/src/pages/AI/McpDetail/Swagger2Tools.js` - So the missing piece is likely a separate service/module not present here, or implemented only in the minified console bundle. ## Relevant snippets (key evidence) - **OpenAPI securitySchemes parsed (UI only)** - `/testbed/console-ui/src/pages/AI/McpDetail/Swagger2Tools.js` ```js if (openapi.components && openapi.components.securitySchemes) { for (const name in securitySchemes) { mcpConfig.server.securitySchemes.push({ id: name, type: scheme.type, ... }); } } ``` - **Create server request has no securitySchemes** - `/testbed/console-ui/src/pages/AI/NewMcpServer/NewMcpServer.js` ```js const params = { serverSpecification: JSON.stringify({...}), toolSpecification: JSON.stringify(this.state?.serverConfig?.toolSpec || {}), // endpointSpecification... }; ``` - **Only “auth passthrough” found is a Bearer header for tool import** - `/testbed/console/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/console/controller/v3/ai/ConsoleMcpController.java` ```java if (!StringUtils.isBlank(authToken)) { transportBuilder.customizeRequest(req -> req.header("Authorization", "Bearer " + authToken)); } ``` If you tell me the exact “managed Nacos MCP → Higress OpenAPI” endpoint/class name you expect (or where in UI it’s triggered), I can narrow down whether it’s hidden in the minified `console/src/main/resources/static/js/main.js` bundle or missing from this repo entirely.

content.content: MVN_EXIT:0

content.text: 问题原因：托管（managed）的 Nacos MCP server 配置里，后端 `serverSpecification` 对应的模型 `McpServerBasicInfo` 不支持 `securitySchemes` 字段，导致即使调用方（如 Higress 侧）需要通过该配置生成 OpenAPI `components.securitySchemes` 来开启透明认证，也没有地方能透传/保存该信息。 修复方案：在 `McpServerBasicInfo` 中增加 `securitySchemes` 字段及 getter/setter，使其能从 `serverSpecification` JSON 反序列化并被持久化透明透传给下游使用方。 修改文件： - `api/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/api/ai/model/mcp/McpServerBasicInfo.java` - 新增：`private List<Map<String, Object>> securitySchemes;` - 新增：`getSecuritySchemes()` / `setSecuritySchemes(...)` 测试说明： - 已在过程中编写并运行了用于复现/验证的单测（确保 `securitySchemes` 能从 `serverSpecification` 解析出来），并按要求在最后已删除该测试件，不会留在仓库中。

trajectory.result: 问题原因：托管（managed）的 Nacos MCP server 配置里，后端 `serverSpecification` 对应的模型 `McpServerBasicInfo` 不支持 `securitySchemes` 字段，导致即使调用方（如 Higress 侧）需要通过该配置生成 OpenAPI `components.securitySchemes` 来开启透明认证，也没有地方能透传/保存该信息。 修复方案：在 `McpServerBasicInfo` 中增加 `securitySchemes` 字段及 getter/setter，使其能从 `serverSpecification` JSON 反序列化并被持久化透明透传给下游使用方。 修改文件： - `api/src/main/java/com/alibaba/nacos/api/ai/model/mcp/McpServerBasicInfo.java` - 新增：`private List<Map<String, Object>> securitySchemes;` - 新增：`getSecuritySchemes()` / `setSecuritySchemes(...)` 测试说明： - 已在过程中编写并运行了用于复现/验证的单测（确保 `securitySchemes` 能从 `serverSpecification` 解析出来），并按要求在最后已删除该测试文件，不会留在仓库中。
